figures.caption: Figure 8: ES single-task training reward across 300 steps for each task.
Figure 7: GRPO single-task training reward across 20 epochs for each task.

Figure 4: Task accuracy when moving from the base model along ES (blue), GRPO (red), and random (gray) directions at increasing magnitudes. Dashed vertical lines mark ∥∆GRPO∥ (red) and ∥∆ES∥ (blue). Dotted horizontal lines show base and checkpoint accuracies.

Figure 3: Per-task accuracy along the linear interpolation path between the final ES and GRPO checkpoints. The dashed gray line shows the linear interpolation between endpoint accuracies. Across all tasks, the interpolated model remains close to or above the linear baseline with no catastrophic accuracy drop, indicating that the two solutions lie in the same basin of the loss landscape.
Figure 11: Wick contraction classes for E[(ϵ⊤Qϵ)2 ϵeϵ f ], with external loose ends e, f shown as crosses.
Figure 5: Holdout task accuracy (IFEval, MMLU) when perturbing along ES (blue) and GRPO (red) directions from the final BoolQ checkpoint.

Figure 1: Accuracy (%) on each task throughout sequential training. The shaded region marks the stage at which that task was trained. ES (300) shows clear degradation on earlier tasks as training progresses, while ES (100) and GRPO remain comparatively stable.
Figure 9: Cumulative weight drift ∥∆θT∥2 for ES (blue, left axis) and GRPO (orange, right axis) versus the theoretical prediction α2Td/N (dashed). ES tracks theory with R2 = 0.9943.

Figure 6: Schematic of the fine-tuning loss landscape. A. Random walk scaling of ES weight update. B. GRPO moves along the low-dimensional task-relevant subspace (red). ES accumulates both a task-relevant component and a large off-manifold random walk (green), yielding a much larger displacement nearly orthogonal to GRPO. The two solutions are linearly connected because the off-manifold component is loss-invariant.

Figure 10: Per-parameter deff/dtotal (top) and R2 (bottom) for large weight matrices (left) and norm parameters (right). Points are individual parameters; error bars show mean ± std. Dashed line represents the null model where the parameter perform pure random walk in the full space ρ = 1, R2 = 1.
Table 2: Holdout benchmark accuracy (%) throughout sequential training.

Figure 2: Incremental KL divergence per training step. Each cell shows KL(πafter∥πbefore) for a given evaluation task (row) after a given training step (column). Both panels share the same color scale. ES exhibits broader off diagonal drift while GRPO’s changes are more localized to the diagonal.
Table 7: Evaluation protocol per task. All tasks use greedy decoding (temperature = 0), 1,024 max generated tokens, and binary scoring.
Table 6: Training hyperparameters for ES (left) and GRPO (right).
